instruction: Questions 21–22: Choose TWO A–E. Questions 23–27: Matching A–G. Questions 28–30: Choose A, B or C.

questions.prompt: Which TWO things do the students agree they need to include in their reviews of Romeo and Juliet?

options.label: analysis of the text
a summary of the plot
a description of the theatre
a personal reaction
a reference to particular scenes

questions.explanation: Phản ứng cá nhân (D) và cảnh cụ thể (E).

questions.prompt: The students think the story of Romeo and Juliet is still relevant for young people today because

options.label: it illustrates how easily conflict can start.
it deals with problems that families experience.
it teaches them about relationships.

questions.sectionInstruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.prompt: The students found watching Romeo and Juliet in another language

options.label: frustrating.
demanding.
moving.

questions.prompt: Why do the students think Shakespeare's plays have such international appeal?

options.label: The stories are exciting.
There are recognisable characters.
They can be interpreted in many ways.
